page: Amazon Web Services r6id.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 128 virtual CPUs (64 physical cores with 2 threads per core) utilizing the x86_64 architecture on an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage consists of 7600 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and the network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 123840.21 and a PassMark CPU score of 87085.72, placing it in the top tier. Memory bandwidth is also strong, reaching 334.55 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 135.74 GB/sec for large block write operations. Database operations are highly efficient, as demonstrated by a PassMark database score of 29749.19 and Redis SET operations reaching 106.91 million operations per second. Single-core performance is average, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1891.9.

From a resource density perspective, the r6id.32xlarge offers high memory capacity alongside substantial local NVMe storage, making it a consolidated platform for data-intensive workloads. The inclusion of 7600 GB of local NVMe SSDs reduces the need for external block storage attachments, optimizing cost efficiency for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space. However, the lack of a dedicated GPU limits its utility for heavy deep learning training, though it achieves strong prompt processing speeds of 194.22 tokens per second for medium 7B LLM inference.

This instance is configured for memory-intensive and storage-heavy applications. Appropriate workloads include large-scale relational and non-relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, data analytics, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r6id.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 128 vCPUs (64 physical cores) on an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 1024.0 GB of DDR4 RAM and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data shows top-tier CPU performance with a PassMark CPU score of 87085.72 and strong memory bandwidth of 334.55 GB/sec for large block reads. Database capabilities are robust, highlighted by 106.91 million Redis SET operations per second. While single-core performance remains average, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and massive local storage provide excellent resource density for data-intensive applications. This instance is suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caches, and enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6id.32xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR4 RAM, and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier CPU and database benchmark performance.

tagline: A memory-optimized platform combining high-capacity DDR4 RAM with massive local NVMe storage for demanding database and data-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors at 3.5 GHz
1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per physical core
7600 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Top-tier PassMark CPU performance and strong Redis database throughput
Optimized for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics
